speaker: DUKE
text: What, is Antonio here?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Ready, so please your Grace.

speaker: DUKE
text: I am sorry for thee. Thou art come to answer

speaker: DUKE
text: A stony adversary, an inhuman wretch,

speaker: DUKE
text: Uncapable of pity, void and empty

speaker: DUKE
text: From any dram of mercy.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I have heard

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Your Grace hath ta’en great pains to qualify

speaker: ANTONIO
text: His rigorous course; but since he stands obdurate,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And that no lawful means can carry me

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Out of his envy’s reach, I do oppose

speaker: ANTONIO
text: My patience to his fury, and am armed

speaker: ANTONIO
text: To suffer with a quietness of spirit

speaker: ANTONIO
text: The very tyranny and rage of his.

speaker: DUKE
text: Go, one, and call the Jew into the court.

speaker: SALERIO
text: He is ready at the door. He comes, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: Make room, and let him stand before our face.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Shylock, the world thinks, and I think so too,

speaker: DUKE
text: That thou but leadest this fashion of thy malice

speaker: DUKE
text: To the last hour of act, and then, ’tis thought,

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou ’lt show thy mercy and remorse more strange

speaker: DUKE
text: Than is thy strange apparent cruelty;

speaker: DUKE
text: And where thou now exacts the penalty,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which is a pound of this poor merchant’s flesh,

speaker: DUKE
text: Thou wilt not only loose the forfeiture,

speaker: DUKE
text: But, touched with humane gentleness and love,

speaker: DUKE
text: Forgive a moi’ty of the principal,

speaker: DUKE
text: Glancing an eye of pity on his losses

speaker: DUKE
text: That have of late so huddled on his back,

speaker: DUKE
text: Enow to press a royal merchant down

speaker: DUKE
text: And pluck commiseration of his state

speaker: DUKE
text: From brassy bosoms and rough hearts of flint,

speaker: DUKE
text: From stubborn Turks, and Tartars never trained

speaker: DUKE
text: To offices of tender courtesy.

speaker: DUKE
text: We all expect a gentle answer, Jew.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I have possessed your Grace of what I purpose,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And by our holy Sabbath have I sworn

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To have the due and forfeit of my bond.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If you deny it, let the danger light

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Upon your charter and your city’s freedom!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You’ll ask me why I rather choose to have

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: A weight of carrion flesh than to receive

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Three thousand ducats. I’ll not answer that,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: But say it is my humor. Is it answered?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: What if my house be troubled with a rat,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And I be pleased to give ten thousand ducats

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To have it baned? What, are you answered yet?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Some men there are love not a gaping pig,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Some that are mad if they behold a cat,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And others, when the bagpipe sings i’ th’ nose,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Cannot contain their urine; for affection

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Masters oft passion, sways it to the mood

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Of what it likes or loathes. Now for your answer:

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: As there is no firm reason to be rendered

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Why he cannot abide a gaping pig,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Why he a harmless necessary cat,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Why he a woolen bagpipe, but of force

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Must yield to such inevitable shame

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: As to offend, himself being offended,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: So can I give no reason, nor I will not,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: More than a lodged hate and a certain loathing

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I bear Antonio, that I follow thus

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: A losing suit against him. Are you answered?

speaker: BASSANIO
text: This is no answer, thou unfeeling man,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: To excuse the current of thy cruelty.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I am not bound to please thee with my answers.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Do all men kill the things they do not love?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Hates any man the thing he would not kill?

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Every offence is not a hate at first.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: What, wouldst thou have a serpent sting thee twice?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I pray you, think you question with the Jew.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: You may as well go stand upon the beach

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And bid the main flood bate his usual height;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: You may as well use question with the wolf

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Why he hath made the ewe bleat for the lamb;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: You may as well forbid the mountain pines

speaker: ANTONIO
text: To wag their high tops and to make no noise

speaker: ANTONIO
text: When they are fretten with the gusts of heaven;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: You may as well do anything most hard

speaker: ANTONIO
text: As seek to soften that than which what’s harder?—

speaker: ANTONIO
text: His Jewish heart. Therefore I do beseech you

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Make no more offers, use no farther means,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: But with all brief and plain conveniency

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Let me have judgment and the Jew his will.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: For thy three thousand ducats here is six.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If every ducat in six thousand ducats

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Were in six parts, and every part a ducat,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I would not draw them. I would have my bond.

speaker: DUKE
text: How shalt thou hope for mercy, rend’ring none?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: What judgment shall I dread, doing no wrong?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You have among you many a purchased slave,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Which, like your asses and your dogs and mules,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You use in abject and in slavish parts

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Because you bought them. Shall I say to you

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: “Let them be free! Marry them to your heirs!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Why sweat they under burdens? Let their beds

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Be made as soft as yours, and let their palates

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Be seasoned with such viands”? You will answer

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: “The slaves are ours!” So do I answer you:

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The pound of flesh which I demand of him

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Is dearly bought; ’tis mine and I will have it.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: If you deny me, fie upon your law:

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: There is no force in the decrees of Venice.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I stand for judgment. Answer: shall I have it?

speaker: DUKE
text: Upon my power I may dismiss this court

speaker: DUKE
text: Unless Bellario, a learnèd doctor

speaker: DUKE
text: Whom I have sent for to determine this,

speaker: DUKE
text: Come here today.

speaker: SALERIO
text: My lord, here stays without

speaker: SALERIO
text: A messenger with letters from the doctor,

speaker: SALERIO
text: New come from Padua.

speaker: DUKE
text: Bring us the letters. Call the messenger.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Good cheer, Antonio! What, man, courage yet!

speaker: BASSANIO
text: The Jew shall have my flesh, blood, bones, and all

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Ere thou shalt lose for me one drop of blood!

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I am a tainted wether of the flock,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Meetest for death. The weakest kind of fruit

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Drops earliest to the ground, and so let me.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: You cannot better be employed, Bassanio,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Than to live still and write mine epitaph.

speaker: DUKE
text: Came you from Padua, from Bellario?

speaker: NERISSA
text: From both, my lord. Bellario greets your Grace.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Why dost thou whet thy knife so earnestly?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To cut the forfeiture from that bankrout there.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Not on thy sole but on thy soul, harsh Jew,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Thou mak’st thy knife keen. But no metal can,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: No, not the hangman’s axe, bear half the keenness

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Of thy sharp envy. Can no prayers pierce thee?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: No, none that thou hast wit enough to make.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: O, be thou damned, inexecrable dog,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: And for thy life let justice be accused;

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Thou almost mak’st me waver in my faith,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: To hold opinion with Pythagoras

speaker: GRATIANO
text: That souls of animals infuse themselves

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Into the trunks of men. Thy currish spirit

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Governed a wolf who, hanged for human slaughter,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Even from the gallows did his fell soul fleet,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: And whilst thou layest in thy unhallowed dam,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Infused itself in thee, for thy desires

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Are wolfish, bloody, starved, and ravenous.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Till thou canst rail the seal from off my bond,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Thou but offend’st thy lungs to speak so loud.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Repair thy wit, good youth, or it will fall

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To cureless ruin. I stand here for law.

speaker: DUKE
text: This letter from Bellario doth commend

speaker: DUKE
text: A young and learnèd doctor to our court.

speaker: DUKE
text: Where is he?

speaker: NERISSA
text: He attendeth here hard by

speaker: NERISSA
text: To know your answer whether you’ll admit him.

speaker: DUKE
text: With all my heart.—Some three or four of you

speaker: DUKE
text: Go give him courteous conduct to this place.

speaker: DUKE
text: Meantime the court shall hear Bellario’s letter.

speaker: DUKE
text: You hear the learnèd Bellario what he writes.

speaker: DUKE
text: And here I take it is the doctor come.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Give me your hand. Come you from old Bellario?

speaker: PORTIA
text: I did, my lord.

speaker: DUKE
text: You are welcome. Take your place.

speaker: DUKE
text: Are you acquainted with the difference

speaker: DUKE
text: That holds this present question in the court?

speaker: PORTIA
text: I am informèd throughly of the cause.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which is the merchant here? And which the Jew?

speaker: DUKE
text: Antonio and old Shylock, both stand forth.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Is your name Shylock?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Shylock is my name.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of a strange nature is the suit you follow,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Yet in such rule that the Venetian law

speaker: PORTIA
text: Cannot impugn you as you do proceed.

speaker: PORTIA
text:
To
                 
                Antonio
                .
                 
              You stand within his danger, do you not?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Ay, so he says.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Do you confess the bond?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I do.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Then must the Jew be merciful.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: On what compulsion must I? Tell me that.

speaker: PORTIA
text: The quality of mercy is not strained.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven

speaker: PORTIA
text: Upon the place beneath. It is twice blest:

speaker: PORTIA
text: It blesseth him that gives and him that takes.

speaker: PORTIA
text: ’Tis mightiest in the mightiest; it becomes

speaker: PORTIA
text: The thronèd monarch better than his crown.

speaker: PORTIA
text: His scepter shows the force of temporal power,

speaker: PORTIA
text: The attribute to awe and majesty

speaker: PORTIA
text: Wherein doth sit the dread and fear of kings;

speaker: PORTIA
text: But mercy is above this sceptered sway.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It is enthronèd in the hearts of kings;

speaker: PORTIA
text: It is an attribute to God Himself;

speaker: PORTIA
text: And earthly power doth then show likest God’s

speaker: PORTIA
text: When mercy seasons justice. Therefore, Jew,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Though justice be thy plea, consider this:

speaker: PORTIA
text: That in the course of justice none of us

speaker: PORTIA
text: Should see salvation. We do pray for mercy,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And that same prayer doth teach us all to render

speaker: PORTIA
text: The deeds of mercy. I have spoke thus much

speaker: PORTIA
text: To mitigate the justice of thy plea,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which, if thou follow, this strict court of Venice

speaker: PORTIA
text: Must needs give sentence ’gainst the merchant there.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: My deeds upon my head! I crave the law,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The penalty and forfeit of my bond.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Is he not able to discharge the money?

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Yes. Here I tender it for him in the court,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Yea, twice the sum. If that will not suffice,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I will be bound to pay it ten times o’er

speaker: BASSANIO
text: On forfeit of my hands, my head, my heart.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: If this will not suffice, it must appear

speaker: BASSANIO
text:
That malice bears down truth.
                 
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Duke
                .
               And I beseech you,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Wrest once the law to your authority.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: To do a great right, do a little wrong,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And curb this cruel devil of his will.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It must not be. There is no power in Venice

speaker: PORTIA
text: Can alter a decree establishèd;

speaker: PORTIA
text: ’Twill be recorded for a precedent

speaker: PORTIA
text: And many an error by the same example

speaker: PORTIA
text: Will rush into the state. It cannot be.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: A Daniel come to judgment! Yea, a Daniel.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: O wise young judge, how I do honor thee!

speaker: PORTIA
text: I pray you let me look upon the bond.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Here ’tis, most reverend doctor, here it is.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Shylock, there’s thrice thy money offered thee.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: An oath, an oath, I have an oath in heaven!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Shall I lay perjury upon my soul?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: No, not for Venice!

speaker: PORTIA
text: Why, this bond is forfeit,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And lawfully by this the Jew may claim

speaker: PORTIA
text: A pound of flesh, to be by him cut off

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nearest the merchant’s heart.—Be merciful;

speaker: PORTIA
text: Take thrice thy money; bid me tear the bond.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: When it is paid according to the tenor.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: It doth appear you are a worthy judge;

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You know the law; your exposition

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Hath been most sound. I charge you by the law,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Whereof you are a well-deserving pillar,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Proceed to judgment. By my soul I swear

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: There is no power in the tongue of man

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To alter me. I stay here on my bond.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Most heartily I do beseech the court

speaker: ANTONIO
text: To give the judgment.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Why, then, thus it is:

speaker: PORTIA
text: You must prepare your bosom for his knife—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: O noble judge! O excellent young man!

speaker: PORTIA
text: For the intent and purpose of the law

speaker: PORTIA
text: Hath full relation to the penalty,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which here appeareth due upon the bond.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: ’Tis very true. O wise and upright judge,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: How much more elder art thou than thy looks!

speaker: PORTIA
text: Therefore lay bare your bosom—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Ay, his breast!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: So says the bond, doth it not, noble judge?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: “Nearest his heart.” Those are the very words.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It is so.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Are there balance here to weigh the flesh?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I have them ready.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Have by some surgeon, Shylock, on your charge,

speaker: PORTIA
text: To stop his wounds, lest he do bleed to death.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Is it so nominated in the bond?

speaker: PORTIA
text: It is not so expressed, but what of that?

speaker: PORTIA
text: ’Twere good you do so much for charity.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I cannot find it. ’Tis not in the bond.

speaker: PORTIA
text: You, merchant, have you anything to say?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: But little. I am armed and well prepared.—

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Give me your hand, Bassanio. Fare you well.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Grieve not that I am fall’n to this for you,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: For herein Fortune shows herself more kind

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Than is her custom: it is still her use

speaker: ANTONIO
text: To let the wretched man outlive his wealth,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: To view with hollow eye and wrinkled brow

speaker: ANTONIO
text: An age of poverty, from which ling’ring penance

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Of such misery doth she cut me off.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Commend me to your honorable wife,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Tell her the process of Antonio’s end,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Say how I loved you, speak me fair in death,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And when the tale is told, bid her be judge

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Whether Bassanio had not once a love.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Repent but you that you shall lose your friend

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And he repents not that he pays your debt.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: For if the Jew do cut but deep enough,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I’ll pay it instantly with all my heart.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Antonio, I am married to a wife

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Which is as dear to me as life itself,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: But life itself, my wife, and all the world

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Are not with me esteemed above thy life.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I would lose all, ay, sacrifice them all

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Here to this devil, to deliver you.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Your wife would give you little thanks for that

speaker: PORTIA
text: If she were by to hear you make the offer.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: I have a wife who I protest I love.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: I would she were in heaven, so she could

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Entreat some power to change this currish Jew.

speaker: NERISSA
text: ’Tis well you offer it behind her back.

speaker: NERISSA
text: The wish would make else an unquiet house.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: These be the Christian husbands! I have a daughter—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Would any of the stock of Barabbas

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Had been her husband, rather than a Christian!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: We trifle time. I pray thee, pursue sentence.

speaker: PORTIA
text: A pound of that same merchant’s flesh is thine:

speaker: PORTIA
text: The court awards it, and the law doth give it.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Most rightful judge!

speaker: PORTIA
text: And you must cut this flesh from off his breast:

speaker: PORTIA
text: The law allows it, and the court awards it.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Most learnèd judge! A sentence!—Come, prepare.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Tarry a little. There is something else.

speaker: PORTIA
text: This bond doth give thee here no jot of blood.

speaker: PORTIA
text: The words expressly are “a pound of flesh.”

speaker: PORTIA
text: Take then thy bond, take thou thy pound of flesh,

speaker: PORTIA
text: But in the cutting it, if thou dost shed

speaker: PORTIA
text: One drop of Christian blood, thy lands and goods

speaker: PORTIA
text: Are by the laws of Venice confiscate

speaker: PORTIA
text: Unto the state of Venice.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: O upright judge!—Mark, Jew.—O learnèd judge!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Is that the law?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thyself shalt see the act.

speaker: PORTIA
text: For, as thou urgest justice, be assured

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thou shalt have justice more than thou desir’st.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: O learnèd judge!—Mark, Jew, a learnèd judge!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I take this offer then. Pay the bond thrice

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And let the Christian go.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Here is the money.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Soft! The Jew shall have all justice. Soft, no haste!

speaker: PORTIA
text: He shall have nothing but the penalty.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: O Jew, an upright judge, a learnèd judge!

speaker: PORTIA
text: Therefore prepare thee to cut off the flesh.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Shed thou no blood, nor cut thou less nor more

speaker: PORTIA
text: But just a pound of flesh. If thou tak’st more

speaker: PORTIA
text: Or less than a just pound, be it but so much

speaker: PORTIA
text: As makes it light or heavy in the substance

speaker: PORTIA
text: Or the division of the twentieth part

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of one poor scruple—nay, if the scale do turn

speaker: PORTIA
text: But in the estimation of a hair,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thou diest, and all thy goods are confiscate.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: A second Daniel! A Daniel, Jew!

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Now, infidel, I have you on the hip.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Why doth the Jew pause? Take thy forfeiture.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Give me my principal and let me go.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I have it ready for thee. Here it is.

speaker: PORTIA
text: He hath refused it in the open court.

speaker: PORTIA
text: He shall have merely justice and his bond.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: A Daniel still, say I! A second Daniel!—

speaker: GRATIANO
text: I thank thee, Jew, for teaching me that word.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Shall I not have barely my principal?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thou shalt have nothing but the forfeiture

speaker: PORTIA
text: To be so taken at thy peril, Jew.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Why, then, the devil give him good of it!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I’ll stay no longer question.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Tarry, Jew.

speaker: PORTIA
text: The law hath yet another hold on you.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It is enacted in the laws of Venice,

speaker: PORTIA
text: If it be proved against an alien

speaker: PORTIA
text: That by direct or indirect attempts

speaker: PORTIA
text: He seek the life of any citizen,

speaker: PORTIA
text: The party ’gainst the which he doth contrive

speaker: PORTIA
text: Shall seize one half his goods; the other half

speaker: PORTIA
text: Comes to the privy coffer of the state,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And the offender’s life lies in the mercy

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of the Duke only, ’gainst all other voice.

speaker: PORTIA
text: In which predicament I say thou stand’st,

speaker: PORTIA
text: For it appears by manifest proceeding

speaker: PORTIA
text: That indirectly, and directly too,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Thou hast contrived against the very life

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of the defendant, and thou hast incurred

speaker: PORTIA
text: The danger formerly by me rehearsed.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Down, therefore, and beg mercy of the Duke.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Beg that thou mayst have leave to hang thyself!

speaker: GRATIANO
text: And yet, thy wealth being forfeit to the state,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Thou hast not left the value of a cord;

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Therefore thou must be hanged at the state’s charge.

speaker: DUKE
text: That thou shalt see the difference of our spirit,

speaker: DUKE
text: I pardon thee thy life before thou ask it.

speaker: DUKE
text: For half thy wealth, it is Antonio’s;

speaker: DUKE
text: The other half comes to the general state,

speaker: DUKE
text: Which humbleness may drive unto a fine.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Ay, for the state, not for Antonio.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Nay, take my life and all. Pardon not that.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: You take my house when you do take the prop

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: That doth sustain my house; you take my life

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: When you do take the means whereby I live.

speaker: PORTIA
text: What mercy can you render him, Antonio?

speaker: GRATIANO
text: A halter gratis, nothing else, for God’s sake!

speaker: ANTONIO
text: So please my lord the Duke and all the court

speaker: ANTONIO
text: To quit the fine for one half of his goods,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: I am content, so he will let me have

speaker: ANTONIO
text: The other half in use, to render it

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Upon his death unto the gentleman

speaker: ANTONIO
text: That lately stole his daughter.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Two things provided more: that for this favor

speaker: ANTONIO
text: He presently become a Christian;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: The other, that he do record a gift,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Here in the court, of all he dies possessed

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Unto his son Lorenzo and his daughter.

speaker: DUKE
text: He shall do this, or else I do recant

speaker: DUKE
text: The pardon that I late pronouncèd here.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Art thou contented, Jew? What dost thou say?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I am content.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Clerk, draw a deed of gift.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I pray you give me leave to go from hence.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I am not well. Send the deed after me

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And I will sign it.

speaker: DUKE
text: Get thee gone, but do it.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: In christ’ning shalt thou have two godfathers.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Had I been judge, thou shouldst have had ten more,

speaker: GRATIANO
text: To bring thee to the gallows, not to the font.

speaker: DUKE
text: Sir, I entreat you home with me to dinner.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I humbly do desire your Grace of pardon.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I must away this night toward Padua,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And it is meet I presently set forth.

speaker: DUKE
text: I am sorry that your leisure serves you not.—

speaker: DUKE
text: Antonio, gratify this gentleman,

speaker: DUKE
text: For in my mind you are much bound to him.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Most worthy gentleman, I and my friend

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Have by your wisdom been this day acquitted

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Of grievous penalties, in lieu whereof

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Three thousand ducats due unto the Jew

speaker: BASSANIO
text: We freely cope your courteous pains withal.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And stand indebted, over and above,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: In love and service to you evermore.

speaker: PORTIA
text: He is well paid that is well satisfied,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And I, delivering you, am satisfied,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And therein do account myself well paid.

speaker: PORTIA
text: My mind was never yet more mercenary.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I pray you know me when we meet again.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I wish you well, and so I take my leave.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Dear sir, of force I must attempt you further.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Take some remembrance of us as a tribute,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Not as fee. Grant me two things, I pray you:

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Not to deny me, and to pardon me.

speaker: PORTIA
text: You press me far, and therefore I will yield.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Give me your gloves; I’ll wear them for your sake—

speaker: PORTIA
text: And for your love I’ll take this ring from you.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Do not draw back your hand; I’ll take no more,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And you in love shall not deny me this.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: This ring, good sir? Alas, it is a trifle.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: I will not shame myself to give you this.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I will have nothing else but only this.

speaker: PORTIA
text: And now methinks I have a mind to it.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: There’s more depends on this than on the value.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: The dearest ring in Venice will I give you,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And find it out by proclamation.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Only for this, I pray you pardon me.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I see, sir, you are liberal in offers.

speaker: PORTIA
text: You taught me first to beg, and now methinks

speaker: PORTIA
text: You teach me how a beggar should be answered.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Good sir, this ring was given me by my wife,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And when she put it on, she made me vow

speaker: BASSANIO
text: That I should neither sell nor give nor lose it.

speaker: PORTIA
text: That ’scuse serves many men to save their gifts.

speaker: PORTIA
text: And if your wife be not a madwoman,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And know how well I have deserved this ring,

speaker: PORTIA
text: She would not hold out enemy forever

speaker: PORTIA
text: For giving it to me. Well, peace be with you.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: My Lord Bassanio, let him have the ring.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Let his deservings and my love withal

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Be valued ’gainst your wife’s commandment.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Go, Gratiano, run and overtake him.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Give him the ring, and bring him if thou canst

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Unto Antonio’s house. Away, make haste.

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Come, you and I will thither presently,

speaker: BASSANIO
text: And in the morning early will we both

speaker: BASSANIO
text: Fly toward Belmont.—Come, Antonio.
